headline: Teenager's AI App Transforms How Citizens Report Road Damage
meta_description: A 15-year-old in New Delhi created an AI-powered app that lets citizens report potholes and automatically drafts letters to authorities.
summary_short: A 15-year-old in New Delhi built an AI app that helps citizens report potholes to government authorities and automatically drafts follow-up letters.
body:
In New Delhi, a 15-year-old has created a solution to a problem that frustrates millions of city dwellers: potholes that go unfixed despite being reported. The app lets citizens document road damage, rate its severity, and submit the information directly to relevant authorities.

What sets this project apart is its intelligent follow-through. Rather than stopping at reporting, the app uses artificial intelligence to draft formal letters and emails to government officials on behalf of users. This removes a major barrier that often prevents complaints from turning into action: the gap between reporting a problem and pushing past bureaucratic delay.

The innovation shows how young people can use technology not just for convenience, but to bridge the gap between citizens and the systems meant to serve them. By automating the tedious work of formal communication, the app makes it easier for people to demand the infrastructure fixes their communities need.
